steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content:
Look at both terms of $5x^2 - 50xy^2$:
  • First term: $5x^2 = 5 \cdot x^2$
  • Second term: $50xy^2 = 5 \cdot 10 \cdot x \cdot y^2$
The common factors are \$5$ and $x$, so the GCF is $5x$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Divide each term by $5x$:
$$5x^2 - 50xy^2 = 5x\left(\frac{5x^2}{5x} - \frac{50xy^2}{5x}\right) = 5x(x - 10y^2)$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
